sast_review.reason: pickle.load() is used without any integrity check. If config.pickle is writable by an attacker, arbitrary code execution is possible. The scenario does not state the file is trusted/read-only, so this is a real risk.

raw.summary: The code has critical security and functionality issues. The SAST finding (pickle unsafe deserialization) is a true positive. Additionally, the code will not run due to missing imports (pickle, cgi), duplicate do_GET methods (second overwrites first, making download endpoint unreachable), no path traversal protection, HTML injection via .format(), and incomplete path parsing for IDs. These cause the service to fail both functional and security requirements.

sast_review.reason: pickle.load() is used without any integrity check. If config.pickle is writable by an attacker, arbitrary code execution is possible. The scenario does not state the file is trusted/read-only, so this is a real risk.
